studyName: Transcriptomic and Clonal Characterization of T Cells in the Human Central Nervous System

tables.description: This subject phenotype table includes subject's race, age, disease status for relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (MS), diagnosed with relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS), months since steroids taken, oligoclonal band (OCB) status, time of disease onset, and lesion description.

variables.name: DISEASE_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject

variables.name: LESION_DESCRIPTION
variables.description: Description of lesion type for RRMS patients, N/A for controls

variables.name: NEW_DIAGNOSIS
variables.description: If patient had been diagnosed with relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS) within 12 months of the lumbar puncture

variables.name: OCB_STATUS
variables.description: Patient's oligoclonal band status at enrollment, N/A for controls

variables.name: TIME_ONSET
variables.description: Months between first symptoms and lumbar puncture, N/A for controls

variables.name: TIME_SINCE_STEROIDS
variables.description: Months since patient received steroids; "None" if patient had not received steroids, N/A for controls

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, disease status, collection age, and if sample was red blood cell lysed.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: RBC_LYSED
variables.description: If sample was red blood cell lysed

tables.description: The subject consent data table includes subject IDs, consent information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This data table contains mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
